content:
Right now in my KMS patch series I'd like to try to remove the reliance on refcounting that we had before sending out another version. I'm not actually sure how much this is going to need HRT, but in general: I'm really confused on how to actually use ForLt for anything? The examples we currently have are not at all enlightening. Hopefully I'm not messing up terminology here, so please let me know if something I'm saying here doesn't sound like it makes sense.
So: the examples don't seem to cover simple questions like "if I have Foo<'a> and I want Foo to be generalized over 'a so I can have a Foo<'static> whose lifetime I can shorten with some &'a Bar, how exactly do I do that safely? Can I not do that safely? And if I can't do that safely, what exactly is supposed does ForLt provide that I wouldn't have without it?". I'm also generally quite confused on where exactly ForLt! should be used.
(also, maybe could we improve the documentation if this ends up getting answered? I'd be happy to help if so)
in general, ForLt really seems to be lacking a lot of documentation
In other words, will there be a workshop/tutorial session about ForLt during Kangrejos/LPC? :)
@Gary Guo @Danilo Krummrich ^

Boqun Feng said:

In other words, will there be a workshop/tutorial session about ForLt during Kangrejos/LPC? :)

I would very mouch appreciate this and would for sure attend.
FWIW too - I think personally I get it, but that's after talking about it at length with Danilo over video call
